disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A pot of clean boiling water tipped down the front of the thigh about an hour ago
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: A pot of boiling water tipped straight down the front of my thigh about an hour ago.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Large intact thin-walled fluid-filled blisters have formed over a red patch about a palm's size
trigger_question_examples: What does the skin look like now?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah, there are three big blisters filling up with clear fluid over a bright red patch about the size of my palm.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Severe continuous burning pain with marked sensitivity to touch and to air moving over the area
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It is blinding — even a draught of air across it makes me flinch, and touching anywhere near it is agony.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Cool running water was applied to the area for about 20 minutes immediately after the injury
trigger_question_examples: What have you done for it so far?
Did you put anything on it?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My wife ran cool tap water over it for about 20 minutes straight away. We haven't put any cream or ice on it.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No part of the area feels numb and none of it looks white, waxy or leathery — it is uniformly red and intensely painful
trigger_question_examples: Is any part of the burn numb or painless?
Does any of it look white, waxy or leathery rather than red?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, none of it is numb — it's all screaming at me. It looks red and blistered, not white or leathery.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The patient is unsure when their last tetanus-containing vaccination was
trigger_question_examples: When was your last tetanus shot?
Are you up to date with your tetanus vaccine?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Honestly I'm not sure — it's probably been years, I can't remember the last one.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The burn was from clean boiling water only — no chemicals, no flame and no electricity were involved
trigger_question_examples: Was it just water, or was any chemical or cleaning product involved?
Was there any flame or electricity?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Just plain boiling water from the pot — no chemicals, no flames, nothing electrical.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No spreading redness beyond the burn, no fever, no chills and no foul discharge at present
trigger_question_examples: Is the redness spreading up the leg?
Do you have any fever, chills or discharge from it?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, the redness is just around the burn, I don't have a fever or chills, and there's no pus or smell — it just happened an hour ago.
